##NAMES=Lithiotantite
##RRUFFID=R100165
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=LiTa_3_O_8_
##LOCALITY=Murundu mine, Jenipapo district, Itinga, Minas Gerais, Brazil
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Luiz Menezes
##DESCRIPTION=Brown bladed crystals associated with reddish brown prismatic tantalite, and black cassiterite. Used in the Menezes et al (2012) study.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R100165
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 7.4589 b: 5.0577 c: 15.269 alpha: 90 beta: 107.372 gamma: 90 volume: 549.7 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
